title: Smoky Light Irish Chicken Roast
description: A healthy, halal‑friendly Irish roast chicken with a smoky spice rub, garlic‑honey glaze, and a quick broil finish for a caramelised crust.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220 °C). Place a rack in the middle and line a baking tray with foil.
In a small bowl, mix smoked paprika, cumin, thyme, salt, pepper, and minced garlic to make the spice rub.
Pat chicken breasts dry with paper towels, then rub the spice mixture all over each piece, pressing gently to adhere.
In a separate bowl, whisk together olive oil, honey, soy sauce, and lemon juice to create the garlic‑honey glaze.
Heat a large oven‑proof skillet over medium‑high heat. Add the chicken breasts skin‑side down until golden, about 3 minutes, then flip.
Pour the glaze over the chicken, spooning it onto the surface, and add the cup of chicken broth to the skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 15–18 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74 °C).
Remove the skillet, increase the oven temperature to broil (top heating element) and broil the chicken for 1–2 minutes to crisp the tops, watching carefully to avoid burning.
While the chicken is broiling, swirl the remaining glaze in a small saucepan over low heat until slightly thickened, then set aside.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes, then serve drizzled with the warmed glaze, accompanied by roasted potatoes or a green salad.
